changes.motivation: Bun was using a shared/global cache that got corrupted (30,067 empty JS files), causing linting errors in worktrees due to broken node_modules. Pinning the cache to a local `.bun` directory at the repo root prevents cross-environment cache corruption.
changes.summary: Created `bunfig.toml` at the repo root to configure Bun's cache directory to `.bun` (local to the repo). Added `.bun/` to `.gitignore` to prevent the local cache from being committed.

problems: The Bun global cache silently accumulated 30,067 empty JS files (vs 3,870 non-empty), causing apparent linting errors in worktrees that didn't reproduce on main. There is no warning or error from Bun when cached files are empty/corrupted — the corruption is invisible until downstream tools (like ESLint) fail.

The repository had no `bunfig.toml` despite managing a large monorepo with worktrees, leaving Bun defaulting to a shared global cache. Sharing a global cache across worktrees and multiple install invocations is fragile. This should have been documented or pre-configured.
